summary: High‑energy Hornsby debut fuels epic Eyes→Playin' jam
blurb: The first set builds solid momentum and hands off to a marathon second set highlighted by a 23‑minute Eyes of the World that segues into a 38‑minute Playing in the Band. Jerry’s soaring leads, Bruce Hornsby's piano fire, and tight drumming drive Around & Around and Johnny B. Goode. The show stands out as one of the most electrifying nights of the 1991 Greensboro run.
review: On March 31, 1991, the Dead rolled into Greensboro Coliseum with a fresh burst of energy courtesy of Bruce Hornsby’s debut on keyboards. From the opening “Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo” through a gritty “Samson & Delilah,” the band was clearly feeling the spark, and reviewers note Jerry Garcia “on fire” with a glowing look during his solos and a soaring Dark Star tease that set the tone for the night. The centerpiece was an almost mythic second‑set arc – a 23‑minute “Eyes Of The World” that melted into a 38‑minute “Playing In The Band,” complete with tight transitions, jazzy turns, and relentless drive from Mickey and Bill that kept the arena pulsing. Around & Around and “Johnny B. Goode” closed the set with classic, high‑octane fun, while the encore “Knockin’ On Heaven’s Door” capped an unforgettable evening. Across all recordings the performance is described as “high‑energy,” “powerful,” and “cohesive,” making this show a must‑listen for anyone craving the Hornsby‑era magic.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of seven recordings are explicitly positive, praising the Hornsby debut, the epic Eyes→Playin' sequence, soaring Garcia leads and overall high‑energy performance. No negative or consistent criticism appears, so a strong positive sentiment (+1.6) is applied.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with balanced mix, crystal‑clear instrument separation and a vivid Hornsby debut that showcases the night’s energy.

key_highlights: Bruce Hornsby's debut adds fresh fire to the band’s groove.
Epic 23‑minute Eyes Of The World flowing into a 38‑minute Playing In The Band jam.
Soaring Dark Star tease and soaring Garcia leads throughout.
Tight Around & Around and rousing Johnny B. Goode finale.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers soaring leads on Half‑Step, Eyes and the long jams, with a standout Dark Star tease that shows his excitement playing alongside Hornsby.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh’s bass is described as mega‑toned and quaking away, providing a solid, energetic foundation for the improvisations.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s rhythm guitar sits prominently in the left channel, offering crisp, reliable comping throughout the set.
band_performance.Brent: Bruce Hornsby’s piano virtuosity (with Vince Welnick adding texture) drives the jams, especially on Eyes and Playing In The Band; both keyboards are forward‑mixed and energize the band.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums start strong with rapid turnarounds but later drift, noted as “dozing off” during the extended second set.
band_performance.Billy: Bill’s drumming mirrors Mickey’s early intensity, though his presence fades toward the end, contributing to a slightly flagging rhythm section.
